# COPILOT_RULES.md

> Coding standards and guidelines for dout.dev development with GitHub Copilot

## Project Philosophy

- **Vanilla-first**: Zero runtime dependencies. Pure HTML5/CSS3/ES2022+ using modern web standards.
- **Semantic HTML**: Element-first approach with minimal classes, leveraging CSS @layer for specificity.
- **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.2 AAA compliance is mandatory, not optional.
- **Performance**: Optimize for Core Web Vitals and real-world conditions.
- **Progressive Enhancement**: Works without JS, better with it.
- **Maintainability**: Clean, readable, well-documented code with CSS design tokens.
- **Language**: Every document, code line, documentation must be in English language
- **File Paths**: All file paths in code must be relative to the script's working directory, never use absolute paths
- **Path Resolution**: All paths in code should be relative to where the script runs from (project root)
- **Import Paths**: Use relative imports like `./config.js`, `../utils.js` etc.
- **File Operations**: Use `process.cwd()` and relative path joins for file system operations
- **Series Support**: CMS & template engine must support `series` front-matter (string or array) producing `series.json` and per-series listing pages.

## Code Style & Formatting

### JavaScript
- Use ES2022+ features where appropriate
- **Node.js imports**: MANDATORY use `node:` prefix for all Node.js built-in modules (e.g., `import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs'`)
- **Package manager**: Use `pnpm` exclusively for dependency management and script execution
- Prefer `const` over `let`, avoid `var`
- Use template literals for string interpolation
- Arrow functions for short callbacks, regular functions for methods
- Use destructuring for cleaner code
- JSDoc comments for all public functions
- **ES Modules**: All scripts must use ES modules with proper imports/exports

## Styleguide and formatter enforcement (MANDATORY)

- Coding rules and styleguide are defined in the context folder `docs/coding-styleguide` (files: `javascript.md`, `css.md`, `html.md`, `a11y.md`) and must be strictly observed for any code or template modification.
- For every file generated by the CMS or template engine (including but not limited to: `.js`, `.css`, `.html`, `.md`, `.json`) it is mandatory to apply the corresponding formatter before commit.
- The formatters to use are those configured in `.vscode/settings.json` of the repository. Currently the configuration includes:
  - `prettier` for HTML, Markdown, JSON
  - `biome` for JavaScript and CSS
  - other formatters specified for file types (e.g. SVG) where present
- The CMS/template-engine generation process must execute (or offer a hook to execute) the formatting of generated files with the above tools (e.g. via `pnpm run format`, `pnpm run format:html`, or direct calls to `prettier`/`biome`) so that output files are always compliant with the project standard.
- The use of the package manager is mandatorily `pnpm`: all dependencies and formatting scripts must be executed with `pnpm`.

These rules are normative: every PR introducing files generated by the template engine must include confirmation that files have been formatted with the indicated formatters and that format tests/validations pass.

### CSS
- **Vanilla CSS only**: Zero CSS frameworks or libraries (no Tailwind, no Bootstrap, no OpenProps)
- **Element/attribute selectors first**: Minimize class usage, prefer semantic selectors
- **CSS @layer API**: Use layers for proper specificity management 
- **Design tokens**: Custom CSS variables following `--target--variation--state--property` naming
- **Mobile-first responsive design** with proper units:
  - `rem` for most measurements (html font-size: 10px base, body: 1.6rem)
  - `em` for typography scaling relative to parent
  - `vh/vw/vmin/vmax` for viewport-relative sizes
  - `ch` for horizontal sizing in typography contexts
  - `lh` for line-height-based measurements
  - `px` only for borders as last resort
- **CSS Hierarchy**: Follow strict property order (variables → position → display → visibility → box-model → colors → typography → transforms → behavior)
- **Logical properties**: Prefer `margin-inline`, `padding-block`, `inset`
- **Modern layout**: Grid and Flexbox with semantic structure

### HTML
- Semantic HTML5 elements
- ARIA landmarks and labels where needed
- Alt text for all images
- Proper heading hierarchy (h1 > h2 > h3...)
- Form labels and fieldsets

## CMS Architecture Rules

- **Modular design**: Keep CMS components in separate modules (post-processor, page-generator, scan, validate, watch, build, config)
- **Export consistency**: Ensure `index.js` exports match actual function names in modules
- **Configuration**: Use unified config system with `defaultConfig` and `resolveConfig` patterns
- **Error handling**: Implement proper error handling with meaningful error messages
- **Module separation**: Each CMS module should have a single responsibility and clear interface
- **Data flow**: Maintain clear data flow from markdown → processed posts → generated pages
- **Template integration**: CMS should work seamlessly with the template engine for page generation

## File Organization

```
./scripts/cms/           # Content management
./scripts/template-engine/ # Template processing
./data/                  # Markdown source
./src/                   # Generated/manual HTML
  ├─ layouts/           # Base layouts
  ├─ components/        # Reusable components
  ├─ templates/         # Page templates (post, tag, month, series)
  ├─ styles/           # CSS files
  └─ scripts/          # Client-side JS
```

### Naming Conventions

- **Files**: kebab-case (`post-card.html`, `main-styles.css`)
- **CSS variables**: `--target--variation--state--property` (e.g., `--button--primary--hover--background`)
- **CSS classes** (when needed): `target--variation--modifier` (e.g., `button--primary--disabled`)
- **JavaScript**: camelCase (`generateSlug`, `processPost`)
- **Constants**: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (`PAGE_SIZE`, `SITE_URL`)
- **HTML attributes**: Custom attributes for component variations (`button[primary]`, `card[featured]`)

## Git Conventions

### Commit Messages
Use Conventional Commits format:

```
type(scope): description

feat(cms): add pagination support for tag pages
fix(a11y): improve skip link focus management
docs(readme): update installation instructions
refactor(template): simplify expression parser
test(cms): add unit tests for post processor
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`

### Branch Names
- `feat/milestone-description` (e.g., `feat/m3-layouts-components`)
- `fix/issue-description` (e.g., `fix/pagination-aria-labels`)
- `docs/topic` (e.g., `docs/template-engine-api`)

## Accessibility Requirements

Every component and page MUST:

- [ ] Use semantic HTML elements appropriately
- [ ] Include proper ARIA labels and landmarks
- [ ] Support keyboard navigation
- [ ] Meet contrast requirements (AAA level)
- [ ] Work with screen readers
- [ ] Respect `prefers-reduced-motion`
- [ ] Include skip links where appropriate
- [ ] Announce dynamic updates (search results, pagination) via aria-live

## Performance Guidelines

- [ ] Lazy load non-critical images
- [ ] Minimize JavaScript bundle size
- [ ] Use critical CSS inlining
- [ ] Optimize Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID)
- [ ] Progressive enhancement approach
- [ ] Efficient asset caching strategies

## Testing & Quality Assurance

### Testing Requirements
**MANDATORY**: Every CMS and template engine functionality MUST have comprehensive test coverage:

- [ ] **Unit tests** for all CMS modules (post-processor, index-generator, page-generator, scan, validate)
- [ ] **Unit tests** for all template engine features (parser, renderer, filters, expression evaluator, template resolution)
- [ ] **Integration tests** for end-to-end CMS workflows (data → HTML generation)
- [ ] **Template tests** for all components and layouts with various data scenarios
- [ ] **Snapshot testing** for generated HTML output consistency
- [ ] **Error handling tests** for all failure scenarios and edge cases

#### Testing Framework & Structure
- **Test framework**: Use Node.js built-in `node:test` framework with `describe/test` structure
- **Test approach**: Follow test-driven development with incremental "one test at a time" approach
- **Test files**: Place tests in `scripts/cms/tests/` directory with `.test.js` extension
- **Module testing**: Test both individual functions and integration scenarios
- **Export consistency**: Ensure `index.js` exports match actual function names in modules

#### Node.js Import Standards
**MANDATORY**: All Node.js built-in module imports must use the `node:` prefix:

```javascript
// ✅ CORRECT
import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs';
import { join, resolve, dirname } from 'node:path';
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';

// ❌ INCORRECT  
import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'fs';
import { join, resolve, dirname } from 'path';
```

#### Package Manager Standards
**MANDATORY**: Use `pnpm` exclusively:

- All scripts must use `pnpm run` instead of `npm run` or `yarn`
- Lock file: `pnpm-lock.yaml` only (remove `yarn.lock`, `package-lock.json`)
- Dependencies installed via `pnpm add` / `pnpm add -D`

#### Test Organization
```
./scripts/cms/tests/           # CMS unit & integration tests
./scripts/template-engine/tests/ # Template engine tests
./tests/                       # E2E and HTML validation tests
```

#### Test Coverage Standards
- **Minimum coverage**: 85% for all critical paths
- **Required test types**: Happy path, error cases, edge cases
- **Test data**: Use realistic test fixtures and data samples
- **CI Integration**: Tests must pass before any merge

#### Completed Work Status

- ✅ **English translation**: All Italian text translated to English
- ✅ **Test coverage**: 30 comprehensive tests across 6 CMS modules

  - `post-processor.test.js`: 8 tests for markdown processing
  - `page-generator.test.js`: 5 tests for HTML generation
  - `scan.test.js`: 5 tests for content scanning
  - `validate.test.js`: 4 tests for content validation
  - `watch.test.js`: 3 tests for file watching
- ✅ **Module creation**: Created missing `validate.js` and `build.js` modules
- ✅ **Export fixes**: Fixed `index.js` exports to match actual function names
- ✅ **CMS Architecture**: Implemented modular design with proper separation of concerns
- ✅ **Testing Infrastructure**: Established Node.js built-in test framework with comprehensive coverage

### HTML Quality & Validation

#### HTML Quality & Validation

#### HTML Generation Standards
**MANDATORY**: The CMS and template engine must generate perfectly formatted HTML:

- [ ] **Root cause validation**: Any HTML formatting issues must be traced back to template engine or CMS source
- [ ] **Template engine output**: All template rendering must produce properly indented, formatted HTML
- [ ] **Component composition**: Include/extend operations must maintain proper formatting
- [ ] **Build pipeline**: CMS generation process must not introduce formatting errors

#### Code Formatting
**MANDATORY**: All generated HTML files must be properly formatted:

- [ ] **Prettier formatting**: All HTML output must be indented and formatted using Prettier
- [ ] **Consistent indentation**: 2 spaces, no tabs
- [ ] **Line length**: Maximum 100 characters where feasible
- [ ] **Proper nesting**: All tags properly nested and closed
- [ ] **Auto-formatting**: Template engine should generate pre-formatted HTML (not rely on post-processing)

#### HTML Validation
**MANDATORY**: Every generated HTML file must pass validation:

- [ ] **HTML5 validation**: No errors using W3C validator or htmlhint
- [ ] **Semantic structure**: Proper heading hierarchy, landmark usage
- [ ] **Accessibility validation**: No axe-core errors
- [ ] **Link validation**: All internal links and assets exist
- [ ] **Meta validation**: Required meta tags present and valid

#### Quality Gates
**BLOCKING**: The following checks must pass before any commit:

```bash
# HTML formatting check
npm run format:check:html

# HTML validation
npm run validate:html

# Structure validation  
npm run validate:structure

# Accessibility check
npm run validate:a11y
```

### CMS Quality Standards

#### Code Generation Standards
- [ ] **Error handling**: Graceful degradation for missing data
- [ ] **Path validation**: All file paths and URLs are valid
- [ ] **Data integrity**: Front matter validation and normalization
- [ ] **Template safety**: No malformed template output
- [ ] **Asset handling**: Images and assets properly copied and referenced

#### Template Engine Standards
- [ ] **Expression safety**: All template expressions properly escaped
- [ ] **Include resolution**: Relative paths resolved correctly
- [ ] **Filter validation**: All filters handle edge cases
- [ ] **Error reporting**: Clear error messages with file/line context
- [ ] **Performance**: No excessive template parsing overhead

## Testing Checklist

Before submitting a PR:

- [ ] Code passes linting (`npm run lint`)
- [ ] **All unit tests pass** (`npm test`)
- [ ] **HTML formatting check passes** (`npm run format:check:html`)
- [ ] **HTML validation passes** (`npm run validate:html`)
- [ ] **Structure validation passes** (`npm run validate:structure`)
- [ ] All accessibility checks pass (`npm run validate:a11y`)
- [ ] Manual keyboard navigation test
- [ ] Screen reader test (VoiceOver/NVDA)
- [ ] Performance budget maintained
- [ ] Works without JavaScript
- [ ] Mobile responsive design verified

### Development Workflow

#### Before Starting Work
1. Run existing tests to ensure baseline functionality
2. Write tests for new functionality (TDD approach preferred)
3. Ensure test coverage meets minimum 85% threshold

#### During Development
1. Run tests frequently (`npm run test:watch`)
2. Check HTML output formatting regularly
3. Validate generated HTML structure
4. Test accessibility with automated tools

#### Before Commit
1. Run full test suite (`npm test`)
2. Format and validate all generated HTML
3. Check coverage reports
4. Validate all links and assets

## Template Engine Guidelines

When working with the custom template engine:

- Use relative paths for includes (`./components/header.html`)
- Always escape user content unless using `| raw` filter
- Keep templates focused and reusable
- Use meaningful block names for layouts
- Document complex template logic

## CMS Development

When modifying the CMS:

- Validate all front matter data
- Handle missing files gracefully
- Provide helpful error messages
- Update indices atomically
- Preserve existing URLs when possible
- Normalize `series` (string | string[]) into deterministic array of objects `{ key, label, url }`
- Generate/update `series.json` with counts and stable ordering
- Produce series listing pages (`./src/series/<series>.html`) extending the base layout

## Browser Support

Target last 2 major versions of:
- Chrome
- Firefox  
- Safari
- Edge

Use progressive enhancement for cutting-edge features. Enhancements (theme switcher, hamburger menu, scroll-spy, search) must degrade gracefully.

## Documentation

- Update README.md for significant changes
- Document new template engine features
- Add JSDoc comments for all public APIs
- Include examples in technical specs
- Keep roadmap updated with progress
- Document series feature (data model, indices, templates)

## Security Considerations

- Sanitize all Markdown input
- Escape HTML by default in templates
- Use CSP headers in production
- Validate file paths (prevent traversal)
- Never use `eval()` or similar dynamic execution

## Development Workflow Guidelines

### Test-First Development
- **Test-first**: Write tests before implementing features
- **Incremental**: Add one test at a time and ensure it passes
- **Integration**: Test both unit functionality and integration between modules
- **Validation**: Run all tests after changes to ensure system stability

### Before Starting Work
1. Run existing tests to ensure baseline functionality
2. Write tests for new functionality (TDD approach preferred)
3. Ensure test coverage meets minimum 85% threshold

### During Development
1. Run tests frequently (`pnpm test:watch`)
2. Check HTML output formatting regularly
3. Validate generated HTML structure
4. Test accessibility with automated tools

### Before Commit
1. Run full test suite (`pnpm test`)
2. Format and validate all generated HTML
3. Check coverage reports
4. Validate all links and assets

---

**Remember**: Accessibility is not a feature to add later - it's a fundamental requirement that must be built in from the start. Series pages and new navigation elements must meet identical accessibility standards.
